name: Operations
description: Cross-machine fleet view for the qontinui-web app. Asserts ONLY data-independent structural chrome confirmed (via a CI element-dump of /operations as ci-bot under both an empty AND a populated coord fleet) to render regardless of runtime data: the page header (h1 + cross-machine subtitle), the cross-repo dependency-graph entry point (heading + inline link + repo/PR inputs + load button), and the Gates panel's data-independent surface (its heading + the 'No gates registered' empty state). The device-status tile is deliberately NOT asserted: the CI dump showed its elements (operations.device-status-tile / heading-2-device-status / refresh / connection) are absent from the spec executor's element registry in BOTH fleet conditions, because the tile is mounted DEEP inside FleetOverview, which only renders the tile in its success/error branches — never in its async loading branch, which is where CI's snapshot lands while the fleet API is still resolving. Asserting any device-status anchor therefore reintroduces a load-state flake. The remaining anchors were present in BOTH dump conditions (empty and populated fleet), so this spec full_matches independent of any live data. Gate ROWS (Approve/Mute/Snooze buttons + plan/claim group headers) are intentionally NOT asserted: Spec-CI runs against a hermetic EMPTY database, so the GatesPanel renders only its heading + empty state — gate rows are absent in CI by construction, and asserting them would always fail. A routeStub pins GET /coord-api/status to an empty fleet purely to replace the CI proxy 500 (no coord in CI) with a benign 200, removing the prior expectedServerErrors waiver.

metadata.routeStubsNote: Pins the device-status tile's GET /coord-api/status (Next same-origin proxy of GET /coord/status) to a deterministic 200 EMPTY fleet. In CI COORD_URL is unset, so the real proxy defaults to http://localhost:9870 with no coord running and 500s; the stub replaces that 5xx with a benign 200, which is why this spec no longer needs the old expectedServerErrors:['/coord-api/status'] waiver. The stub does NOT affect this spec's assertions (none target the device-status tile) — it is purely a server-error-policy hygiene measure.

states.name: Page header
states.description: Top-of-page banner - always rendered regardless of backend availability or fleet state. Both anchors confirmed present in the CI dump under empty AND populated fleet.

assertions.description: h1 Operations heading anchors the page.
Cross-machine subtitle paragraph anchors the page's purpose.

states.name: Cross-repo dependency-graph entry point
states.description: Operator-driven cross-repo dependency-graph mini-form (PR Merge Orchestrator Phase 5 D5.5). Always rendered - no auth gating, no data dependency. All five anchors confirmed present in the CI dump under empty AND populated fleet.

assertions.description: Section h4 names the cross-repo dependencies panel.
Inline link routes to the full dependency-graph view.
Repo (owner/name) text input is present so the operator can select a connected component.
PR number input completes the (repo, pr) tuple.
Submit button loads the connected component into the embedded graph.

states.name: Gates panel (empty-state surface)
states.description: Tenant-scoped gates read/manage panel (plan 2026-06-05-plan-gate-web-surface-and-productization Phase 2). Asserts ONLY the data-independent surface that renders against a hermetic EMPTY database: the panel heading and the 'No gates registered' empty state. Gate ROWS and their Approve/Mute/Snooze action buttons render only when the tenant has gates — ABSENT in CI by construction (empty coord) — so they are deliberately NOT asserted here. Both anchors carry explicit data-content-id attributes (heading-gates / gates-empty-state) so the ids are text-independent and stable across copy edits.

assertions.description: Gates panel heading anchors the gate read/manage surface; rendered regardless of gate data.
Empty-state paragraph renders when no gates are registered — the only gate-list content present in CI's hermetic empty database.
